title: Machinery external-checker evidence (checker -> machinery)
description: What an external checker, or an adapter the checker author ships, writes back for machinery to verify. Machinery's PURE check phase (`machinery check --gate gk`) reads ONLY this file: it never parses the checker's native output, so a checker may emit anything internally (a SARIF report, a signed decision, a converged interval graph) and map it here. The verdict is trusted in the pure phase only because `input_hash` binds it to an exact projection; the separate external phase (`machinery verify-checkers`) re-runs or replays the engine to confirm the verdict was earned. Fields under `generated` are non-binding.

id.description: MUST equal the manifest checker id and the projection `checker_id`. A mismatch is an ERROR: this evidence was produced for a different checker.

version.description: The engine and/or ruleset version. Surfaced in a non-blocking skew note when it changes between runs; not part of the binding.

input_hash.description: THE BINDING. MUST equal machinery's sha256 over the canonical projection bytes (the projection's `generated` object excluded). A verdict whose input_hash does not match a fresh projection is DRIFT: it was computed over a different design. This single field is what makes a verdict from an opaque engine safe to trust in the pure phase.

verdict.description: pass: the checker affirms every claimed element it decided, with no blocking finding. fail: at least one blocking finding. An element the checker could NOT decide is not expressed here; it becomes a coverage gap, resolved by machinery against the manifest claim.

coverage.description: One row per design element the checker actually decided, each keyed by a projection stable_id. Machinery cross-checks this list against the manifest's coverage.claim: a claimed element that is absent here and is not a declared residual FAILS the gate. This is the 'every feature, every time' hard rule, reused from the relational-layer residuals pattern. May be empty only when the manifest claims nothing.

element.description: A stable_id present in the projection this evidence binds to.

findings.description: Machinery surfaces these: `blocking` severities become ERRORs on a fail verdict; `advisory` become warns; `info` become notes. Empty on a clean pass.

code.description: A stable checker-defined code: a control id, a rule name, a lint id. Lets machinery de-duplicate and lets you triage.

element.description: Optional projection stable_id this finding is about.

locator.description: Optional pointer into the checker's own source (rule file:line, a norm id, an AST path). Machinery treats it as opaque text.

input_signature.description: OPTIONAL. A detached signature OVER the ASCII bytes of `input_hash`, in a scheme the registry declares, that machinery's PURE phase verifies (crypto is deterministic, so this strengthens `check` without running the engine). Present only for checkers that sign their input binding directly. A checker that signs its own NATIVE artifact instead, in a format machinery does not parse, leaves this ABSENT and carries provenance under `attestation`, verified in the external phase by the checker's own verify command.

value.description: Base64 detached signature over the exact ASCII bytes of input_hash.

key_id.description: Which public key, from the registry entry's declared keys, verifies this.

attestation.description: OPTIONAL and OPAQUE to the pure phase. A free-form reference to the checker's native, possibly-signed artifacts: a compiled-ruleset hash, a signed decision, a replayable trace, an approval record. Machinery does NOT interpret it. The registry's `verify` command consumes it in the external phase to re-run or replay the engine. This is how a checker that emits what it emits, and cannot be changed, carries its own provenance without machinery ever learning its format.

trace_ref.description: OPTIONAL path (relative to this evidence file) to the checker's native trace or proof artifact, for the external verify phase to replay. Opaque to the pure phase.

generated.description: NON-BINDING provenance (timestamps, host, wall-clock). Excluded from every freshness comparison.
